//: Trail opt-in hooks. Review before pasting into ~/.claude/settings.json.
//1: PreToolUse guard-tag: blocks `git tag phase/*` when the capsule
//2: annotation is incomplete for the phase's declared risk level, when
//3: the tag name doesn't follow phase/<feature-slug>-N, or when a prior
//4: phase's audit is still pending. Silent on every other Bash invocation.
//6: Stop trace_status: prints current Trail state at session end. The
//7: script self-suppresses on non-Trail repos so non-Trail sessions stay quiet.
//9: Paths use $HOME (not ~) for portability across hook runners that
//10: may not perform tilde expansion. If your runner doesn't expand $HOME
//11: either, replace with the absolute path to your home directory.
//13: Debug: set TRAIL_HOOK_DEBUG=1 in the environment to log raw hook
//14: payloads to stderr — useful if the hook seems silent when it shouldn't be.
//16: Trust note: opt-in hooks are functionally equivalent to allowed-tools
//17: from a trust perspective. You are granting these scripts permission to
//18: execute on every matching tool call. Review the script source under
